pattern: Validating a level-alternating parity-and-monotonic rule is naturally a level-by-level traversal: each level only needs to remember the previous value seen at that same depth to check both parity and strict ordering as it goes.
hints: Process the tree one full level at a time rather than node by node, since every rule (parity, and whether values increase or decrease) is scoped to a single level.
On an even-indexed level (0, 2, 4, ...) every value must be odd and strictly increasing left to right; on an odd-indexed level every value must be even and strictly decreasing left to right.

Walk the tree level by level with a queue, tracking the previous value seen so far at the current level (reset to none at the start of each new level) so both the parity and ordering checks are simple comparisons against it.

edge_inputs.description: a tree that fails because a value at an odd level is odd instead of even
an even-level value that is not strictly increasing from its left sibling
an odd-level value that is not strictly decreasing from its left sibling
a single even value at the root fails immediately since the root must be odd
a lopsided tree where one branch is much deeper than the other

stress.description: randomly generated trees, some valid even-odd trees and some deliberately broken
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    depth = rng.randint(1, 8)
    values = []
    level_prev = None
    nodes = 0
    max_nodes = max(1, min(size, 2 ** depth - 1))
    level = 0
    level_size = 1
    idx_in_level = 0
    for _ in range(max_nodes):
        if idx_in_level == 0:
            level_prev = None
        if level % 2 == 0:
            v = (level_prev + 2) if level_prev is not None else rng.choice([1, 3, 5])
        else:
            v = (level_prev - 2) if level_prev is not None and level_prev - 2 >= 2 else 2
        if rng.random() < 0.15:
            v += 1
        values.append(v)
        level_prev = v
        idx_in_level += 1
        if idx_in_level == level_size:
            level += 1
            level_size *= 2
            idx_in_level = 0
    return [values]
